theme: Ordenar palavras para formar frases

exercises.instruction_pt: Coloque as palavras na ordem correta:
words: is
name
My
Maria
exercises.correct_sentence: My name is Maria.
exercises.explanation_pt: Ordem: Sujeito + Verbo + Complemento. 'My name' (meu nome) + 'is' (é) + 'Maria'.

exercises.instruction_pt: Coloque as palavras na ordem correta:
words: live
I
in
Brazil
exercises.correct_sentence: I live in Brazil.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'I' (eu) + 'live' (moro) + 'in' (em) + 'Brazil'.

exercises.instruction_pt: Coloque as palavras na ordem correta:
words: is
He
a
doctor
exercises.correct_sentence: He is a doctor.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'He' (ele) + 'is' (é) + 'a' (um) + 'doctor' (médico).

exercises.instruction_pt: Coloque as palavras na ordem correta:
words: like
I
coffee
don't
exercises.correct_sentence: I don't like coffee.
exercises.explanation_pt: Negativa: Sujeito + don't + verbo. 'I don't like coffee' = Eu não gosto de café.

exercises.instruction_pt: Coloque as palavras na ordem correta:
words: the
Where
hospital
is
exercises.correct_sentence: Where is the hospital?
exercises.explanation_pt: Pergunta com 'where': Where + is + sujeito. 'Where is the hospital?' = Onde fica o hospital?

theme: Completar frases

exercises.instruction_pt: Complete a frase com a palavra correta:
exercises.sentence: She _____ to school every day.
exercises.explanation_pt: Com she/he/it no presente simples, o verbo recebe '-s': She goes.

exercises.instruction_pt: Complete a frase:
exercises.sentence: They _____ watching TV right now.
exercises.explanation_pt: Com 'they' usamos 'are'. They are watching = Eles estão assistindo.

exercises.instruction_pt: Complete a frase:
exercises.sentence: I _____ born in 1999.
exercises.explanation_pt: Para passado com 'I', usamos 'was'. I was born = Eu nasci.

exercises.instruction_pt: Complete a frase:
exercises.sentence: We need to _____ at 6 AM tomorrow.
exercises.explanation_pt: Depois de 'need to', usamos o verbo no infinitivo: need to wake up.

exercises.instruction_pt: Complete a frase:
exercises.sentence: There _____ a lot of people at the party.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'People' é plural, então usamos 'were'. There were = Havia (plural).

theme: Escrever frases descrevendo cenas

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva uma frase descrevendo a cena. Use o modelo sugerido.
exercises.scene_pt: Um gato dormindo em cima de um sofá.
exercises.model_answer: The cat is sleeping on the sofa.

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva uma frase:
exercises.model_answer: The children are playing in the park.

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva uma frase:
exercises.scene_pt: Uma mulher lendo um livro na biblioteca.
exercises.model_answer: The woman is reading a book in the library.

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva uma frase:
exercises.model_answer: The man is cooking in the kitchen.

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva uma frase:
exercises.scene_pt: Está chovendo e as pessoas estão usando guarda-chuvas.
exercises.template: It is _____ and people are using _____.
exercises.model_answer: It is raining and people are using umbrellas.

theme: Escrever 2-3 frases respondendo perguntas

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva 2-3 frases respondendo a pergunta:
exercises.prompt: What do you do every morning?
exercises.hint_pt: Use: I wake up... I brush... I eat... I go to...
exercises.model_answer: Every morning, I wake up at 7 o'clock. I brush my teeth and take a shower. Then I have breakfast and go to school.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Presente simples: I wake, I brush, I go

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva 2-3 frases:
exercises.prompt: Describe your best friend.
exercises.hint_pt: Use: My best friend is... He/She is... He/She likes...
exercises.model_answer: My best friend is Lucas. He is 24 years old and studies engineering. He is funny and likes playing soccer on weekends.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Presente simples + adjetivos: He is tall, She is kind

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva 2-3 frases:
exercises.prompt: What did you do last weekend?
exercises.hint_pt: Use: Last weekend I... On Saturday I... On Sunday I...
exercises.model_answer: Last weekend, I visited my grandparents. On Saturday, we had lunch together and talked about family stories. On Sunday, I stayed home and watched a movie.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Passado simples: I visited, I watched, I stayed

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva 2-3 frases:
exercises.prompt: What is your favorite food and why?
exercises.hint_pt: Use: My favorite food is... I like it because... I usually eat it...
exercises.model_answer: My favorite food is pizza. I like it because it has many different flavors. I usually eat pizza on Friday nights with my family.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Presente simples + because (porque)

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva 2-3 frases:
exercises.prompt: Describe the weather today.
exercises.hint_pt: Use: Today the weather is... It is... The temperature is...
exercises.model_answer: Today the weather is sunny and warm. It is a perfect day to go outside. The temperature is about 28 degrees.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Presente simples + adjetivos de clima: sunny, rainy, cold

theme: Escrever um parágrafo curto sobre você

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva um parágrafo curto (3-4 frases) se apresentando em inglês:
exercises.prompt: Introduce yourself: name, age, where you live, what you study/do.
exercises.hint_pt: Use: My name is... I am... years old. I live in... I study/work...
exercises.model_answer: My name is Pedro and I am 22 years old. I live in Ribeirão Preto, a city in the state of São Paulo. I am a medical student at the university. I want to do an internship abroad, so I am studying English.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Presente simples para fatos sobre você: I am, I live, I study, I want

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva 3-4 frases sobre sua rotina diária:
exercises.prompt: Describe your daily routine from morning to night.
exercises.hint_pt: Use: In the morning I... In the afternoon I... In the evening I... I go to bed at...
exercises.model_answer: In the morning, I wake up at 6:30 and go to university. My classes finish at noon. In the afternoon, I study at the library and practice English for one hour. In the evening, I have dinner with my family and go to bed at 11 PM.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Presente simples + expressões de tempo: in the morning, at noon, in the evening

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva 3-4 frases sobre sua cidade:
exercises.prompt: Describe the city where you live.
exercises.hint_pt: Use: I live in... It is a... city. It has... The weather is...
exercises.model_answer: I live in Campinas, which is a large city in São Paulo state. It has many universities and a good public transportation system. The weather is warm most of the year. I like living here because there are many parks and cultural events.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Presente simples + there is/are + adjetivos

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva 3-4 frases sobre por que você quer aprender inglês:
exercises.prompt: Why do you want to learn English?
exercises.hint_pt: Use: I want to learn English because... I need it for... In the future, I want to...
exercises.model_answer: I want to learn English because I need it for my career. As a medical student, I want to do an internship at a hospital in the United States. English is also important for reading scientific papers and attending international conferences. In the future, I hope to work in both Brazil and abroad.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Want to + infinitivo, need it for, hope to

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva 3-4 frases sobre seu hobby favorito:
exercises.prompt: What is your favorite hobby and why do you enjoy it?
exercises.hint_pt: Use: My favorite hobby is... I enjoy it because... I usually do it...
exercises.model_answer: My favorite hobby is playing guitar. I enjoy it because it helps me relax after a long day of studying. I usually play for about 30 minutes before dinner. I have been playing since I was 15 years old.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Presente simples + because + present perfect (I have been)

theme: Escrever sobre sua família

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva 3-4 frases sobre sua família:
exercises.prompt: Describe your family. How many people are in your family? What do they do?
exercises.hint_pt: Use: My family has... My mother/father is... My brother/sister...
exercises.model_answer: My family has five people: my parents, my two brothers, and me. My father is a lawyer and my mother is a nurse. My older brother is studying engineering at university, and my younger brother is still in high school. We all live together in a house in the suburbs.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Presente simples para descrever pessoas: My father is..., My mother works...

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva 3-4 frases:
exercises.prompt: What do you and your family do on weekends?
exercises.hint_pt: Use: On weekends, we usually... Sometimes we... My favorite activity is...
exercises.model_answer: On weekends, my family usually has lunch together at home. After lunch, my parents like to watch movies while my brothers and I play video games. Sometimes we go to the park or visit our grandparents. My favorite weekend activity is cooking with my mother.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Presente simples + advérbios de frequência: usually, sometimes, always

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva 3-4 frases:
exercises.prompt: Describe a family tradition that is important to you.
exercises.hint_pt: Use: Every [occasion], my family... We always... This tradition is important because...
exercises.model_answer: Every Christmas, my family gathers at my grandmother's house. We always cook a big dinner together and exchange gifts at midnight. After dinner, we sit in the living room and share stories from the past year. This tradition is important to me because it brings our whole family together.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Presente simples para hábitos + because para dar razões

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva 3-4 frases:
exercises.prompt: Who is your favorite family member and why?
exercises.hint_pt: Use: My favorite family member is... because... He/She always... I admire...
exercises.model_answer: My favorite family member is my grandmother. She is 78 years old but has more energy than anyone I know. She always tells me interesting stories about when she was young. I admire her because she is very strong and never complains about anything.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Presente simples + superlativos e comparativos

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva 3-4 frases:
exercises.prompt: How is your family similar to or different from a typical Brazilian family?
exercises.hint_pt: Use: My family is similar/different because... Unlike most families... Like many families...
exercises.model_answer: Like many Brazilian families, we eat rice and beans almost every day and we are very close to our extended family. However, unlike most families in our neighborhood, both my parents work full-time and we don't have lunch together during the week. We are also smaller than average — most of our neighbors have three or four children.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Comparações: similar to, different from, unlike, like

theme: Escrever parágrafos de opinião

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva um parágrafo (4-5 frases) dando sua opinião:
exercises.prompt: Do you think university education should be free? Why or why not?
exercises.hint_pt: Estrutura: 1) Sua opinião 2) Razão 1 3) Razão 2 4) Contra-argumento 5) Conclusão
exercises.model_answer: I believe university education should be free for everyone. First, many talented students cannot afford tuition fees and miss the opportunity to develop their potential. Second, countries with free education, like Germany and Finland, have lower inequality and stronger economies. Some people argue that free education would be too expensive for the government, but I think it is an investment that pays for itself through higher tax revenues from educated workers. In conclusion, free education benefits both individuals and society.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Estrutura de opinião: I believe/think + First... Second... In conclusion...

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva um parágrafo de opinião:
exercises.prompt: Is social media more harmful or helpful for teenagers?
exercises.hint_pt: Dê sua opinião + 2 razões + 1 contra-argumento + conclusão
exercises.model_answer: In my opinion, social media is more harmful than helpful for teenagers. The constant comparison with others can cause anxiety and low self-esteem. Additionally, many teenagers spend hours scrolling through social media instead of studying or exercising. While it is true that social media helps young people stay connected with friends, I think the negative effects outweigh the benefits. Parents and schools should set limits on social media use.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Conectores de opinião: In my opinion, Additionally, While it is true that, I think

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva um parágrafo de opinião:
exercises.prompt: Should students be required to wear school uniforms?
exercises.hint_pt: Opinião + razões a favor OU contra + conclusão
exercises.model_answer: I think school uniforms are a good idea for several reasons. First, they reduce social pressure and bullying related to clothing and brands. When everyone wears the same outfit, students focus more on learning and less on appearances. Second, uniforms save families money because they don't need to buy expensive trendy clothes. However, I understand that some students feel uniforms limit their self-expression. Overall, I believe the benefits of uniforms outweigh the drawbacks.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Estrutura: I think... First... Second... However... Overall...

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva um parágrafo de opinião:
exercises.prompt: Is it better to study abroad or in your home country?
exercises.hint_pt: Opinião + vantagens + desvantagens + conclusão pessoal
exercises.model_answer: I believe studying abroad is a valuable experience, but it is not necessarily better for everyone. Studying in another country improves your language skills, exposes you to different cultures, and helps you become more independent. On the other hand, studying abroad is expensive, and being far from family can be emotionally challenging. For students who can afford it and are ready for the challenge, I would recommend at least one semester abroad. However, excellent education is also available in Brazil.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Contraposição: I believe... On the other hand... However...

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva um parágrafo de opinião:
exercises.prompt: Should the voting age be lowered to 16?
exercises.hint_pt: Opinião clara + 2 argumentos + contra-argumento + conclusão
exercises.model_answer: I do not think the voting age should be lowered to 16. At that age, most teenagers are still developing their understanding of politics and may be easily influenced by their parents or social media. Furthermore, 16-year-olds do not pay taxes or have the same responsibilities as adults, so they should not have the same rights. Some people argue that young people deserve a voice in decisions that affect their future, and I agree with that sentiment. However, I think there are better ways for teenagers to participate in politics, such as student councils and community projects.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Argumentação: I do not think... Furthermore... Some people argue... However...

theme: Descrever processos

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva um parágrafo descrevendo um processo passo a passo:
exercises.prompt: Describe the process of applying to a university in Brazil.
exercises.hint_pt: Use: First... Then... Next... After that... Finally...
exercises.model_answer: The process of applying to a university in Brazil usually begins with studying for the ENEM exam, which is held once a year in November. First, students register for the exam on the INEP website, usually between May and June. Then, they spend several months preparing, often attending preparatory courses. Next, they take the two-day exam, which covers subjects like math, science, languages, and writing. After the results are released in January, students use their scores to apply to universities through the SISU system. Finally, they wait for the selection results and, if accepted, complete the enrollment process at the chosen university.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Sequência: First → Then → Next → After that → Finally

exercises.instruction_pt: Descreva o processo:
exercises.prompt: How do you prepare for a job interview?
exercises.hint_pt: Descreva cada etapa desde a preparação até o dia da entrevista
exercises.model_answer: Preparing for a job interview requires several important steps. First, you should research the company thoroughly — learn about their products, values, and recent news. Then, review the job description carefully and prepare examples of how your skills match what they're looking for. Next, practice answering common interview questions out loud, either alone or with a friend. The day before the interview, choose your outfit and plan your route to arrive on time. Finally, on the day of the interview, arrive ten minutes early, bring copies of your resume, and remember to make eye contact and speak clearly.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Imperativo para instruções: research, review, practice + sequenciadores

exercises.instruction_pt: Descreva o processo:
exercises.prompt: Explain how to open a bank account in Brazil.
exercises.hint_pt: Descreva os documentos necessários e os passos
exercises.model_answer: Opening a bank account in Brazil is a relatively simple process. First, you need to gather the required documents: your CPF, RG or passport, proof of address, and proof of income. Then, choose a bank that offers the best services for your needs — compare fees, interest rates, and available apps. Next, visit a branch or, in many cases, complete the application online through the bank's app. After submitting your documents, the bank will verify your information, which usually takes one to three business days. Finally, once approved, you will receive your account number, debit card, and access to online banking.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Voz passiva: documents are required, information will be verified

exercises.instruction_pt: Descreva o processo:
exercises.prompt: Describe the steps to write a good essay.
exercises.hint_pt: Desde a escolha do tema até a revisão final
exercises.model_answer: Writing a good essay involves several key steps. First, carefully read and understand the essay prompt to know exactly what is being asked. Then, brainstorm ideas and create an outline with your main points. Next, write your introduction, starting with a hook to grab the reader's attention, followed by your thesis statement. After that, develop each main point in separate body paragraphs, using evidence and examples to support your arguments. Then, write a conclusion that summarizes your points and restates your thesis. Finally, proofread your essay for grammar, spelling, and punctuation errors, and make sure your ideas flow logically from one paragraph to the next.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Imperativo + gerúndio: Writing involves... starting with... followed by...

exercises.instruction_pt: Descreva o processo:
exercises.prompt: Explain the steps to plan a trip abroad.
exercises.hint_pt: Desde a escolha do destino até a viagem
exercises.model_answer: Planning a trip abroad requires careful preparation. First, choose your destination based on your budget, interests, and the time of year you plan to travel. Then, check if you need a visa and apply for one well in advance, as the process can take weeks. Next, book your flights and accommodation — comparing prices on different websites can save you a lot of money. After that, purchase travel insurance to protect yourself against unexpected events like illness or lost luggage. You should also research local customs, exchange rates, and must-see attractions. Finally, make copies of your passport and important documents, and download useful apps like translation tools and maps that work offline.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Modais: You should, you need to, you must + imperativo

theme: Escrever parágrafos de comparação

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva um parágrafo comparando duas coisas:
exercises.prompt: Compare city life and country life. Which do you prefer?
exercises.hint_pt: Use: Both... However... While... On the other hand... In my opinion...
exercises.model_answer: City life and country life each have their own advantages and disadvantages. Cities offer more job opportunities, better healthcare, and a wider variety of entertainment options such as restaurants, theaters, and shopping centers. On the other hand, the countryside provides a quieter and more peaceful environment, cleaner air, and a stronger connection to nature. While cities tend to be more stressful due to traffic, noise, and high costs of living, rural areas can feel isolating for people who enjoy social activities. In my opinion, city life is better for young professionals who are building their careers, while country life is ideal for families and retirees who value tranquility.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Comparação: Both... While... On the other hand... In my opinion...

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva um parágrafo de comparação:
exercises.prompt: Compare learning English through apps and learning with a teacher.
exercises.hint_pt: Vantagens e desvantagens de cada um + sua preferência
exercises.model_answer: Both learning English through apps and learning with a teacher have their strengths. Apps like Duolingo offer convenience — you can study anytime, anywhere, and at your own pace. They are also much cheaper than hiring a private teacher. However, a teacher can provide personalized feedback, correct your pronunciation in real time, and adapt lessons to your specific needs. While apps are excellent for building vocabulary and practicing grammar, they cannot replicate the human interaction that is essential for developing speaking and listening skills. I believe the most effective approach is a combination of both: using apps for daily practice and having a teacher for weekly conversation sessions.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Comparação com While... However... I believe...

exercises.instruction_pt: Compare:
exercises.prompt: Compare public transportation and private cars.
exercises.hint_pt: Custo, conveniência, meio ambiente, tempo
exercises.model_answer: Public transportation and private cars serve the same purpose — getting people from one place to another — but they differ significantly in cost, convenience, and environmental impact. Public transportation is much cheaper than owning a car, especially when you consider fuel, insurance, maintenance, and parking costs. It is also better for the environment, as buses and trains produce fewer emissions per passenger than individual cars. However, private cars offer more flexibility and comfort. You can go wherever you want, whenever you want, without waiting for schedules. For people who live in cities with good public transit systems, I think buses and trains are the better choice. But for those in rural areas with limited options, a car may be necessary.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Comparação estruturada: differ in... However... For people who... But for those...

exercises.instruction_pt: Compare:
exercises.prompt: Compare traditional classrooms and online learning.
exercises.hint_pt: Interação, flexibilidade, custo, eficácia
exercises.model_answer: Traditional classrooms and online learning represent two different approaches to education, each with distinct advantages. Traditional classrooms provide face-to-face interaction, immediate feedback from teachers, and a structured learning environment that helps students stay focused. Classmates can collaborate on projects and learn from each other's questions. Online learning, in contrast, offers greater flexibility — students can watch lectures at any time and review material as many times as needed. It is often more affordable and eliminates commuting time. However, online students may feel isolated and struggle with self-discipline. Research suggests that the best results come from a blended approach that combines the structure of traditional classes with the flexibility of online resources.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Contraste: in contrast, however + conclusão com blended approach

exercises.instruction_pt: Compare:
exercises.prompt: Compare the TOEFL and IELTS exams.
exercises.hint_pt: Formato, aceitação, dificuldade, custo
exercises.model_answer: The TOEFL and IELTS are the two most widely accepted English proficiency exams in the world, but they have important differences. The TOEFL is entirely computer-based and uses American English, while the IELTS offers both a computer-based and paper-based option and uses British English. The TOEFL speaking section is recorded on a computer, whereas the IELTS speaking test is conducted face-to-face with an examiner. In terms of acceptance, American universities generally prefer TOEFL, while British and Australian institutions tend to favor IELTS. Both exams cost approximately the same amount. I personally find the TOEFL more challenging because you need to type your essays and speak to a computer, which feels unnatural. However, the best exam for you depends on where you plan to study.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Comparação formal: while, whereas, in terms of, both... However...

theme: Escrever ensaios curtos (2 parágrafos)

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva um ensaio curto (2 parágrafos) com introdução e conclusão:
exercises.prompt: Should medical school be free in Brazil? Give reasons for your answer.
exercises.hint_pt: Parágrafo 1: Introdução + argumentos a favor. Parágrafo 2: Contra-argumento + sua conclusão.
exercises.model_answer:
Medical education in Brazil is already free at public universities, but the competition to get in is extremely fierce. I believe that more spots in free medical programs should be created. Doctors play an essential role in society, and many talented students from low-income families cannot afford private medical schools, which charge over R$10,000 per month. By expanding free medical education, the government would increase the number of doctors in underserved regions and reduce health inequalities.

Some people argue that medical education is expensive and the government cannot afford to create more free spots. While this is a valid concern, the long-term benefits outweigh the costs. A healthier population is more productive, which generates more tax revenue. Countries like Cuba and Argentina offer free medical education and have excellent healthcare indicators. In conclusion, investing in free medical education is not just a social responsibility — it is an economic investment in the country's future.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Ensaio: Introdução com tese → Argumentos com evidência → Contra-argumento → Conclusão

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva um ensaio curto (2 parágrafos):
exercises.prompt: Is technology making us more connected or more isolated? Discuss.
exercises.hint_pt: Parágrafo 1: Como a tecnologia conecta. Parágrafo 2: Como pode isolar + sua conclusão.
exercises.model_answer:
Technology has undeniably made communication easier and faster than ever before. Social media platforms allow us to stay in touch with friends and family across the globe, video calls make long-distance relationships more manageable, and online communities connect people who share similar interests but live in different countries. In many ways, technology has broken down geographical barriers and created a more connected world.

However, this digital connectivity often comes at the expense of meaningful face-to-face interaction. Many people spend hours scrolling through social media feeds instead of having real conversations with the people sitting next to them. Studies have shown that heavy social media use is linked to increased feelings of loneliness and depression, particularly among young adults. In my view, technology itself is neither good nor bad — what matters is how we use it. If we use it as a supplement to real-world relationships rather than a replacement, it can truly bring us closer together.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Ensaio argumentativo: tese + evidências + contra-argumento + conclusão pessoal

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva um ensaio curto (2 parágrafos):
exercises.prompt: What is the most important skill for success in the 21st century? Explain your choice.
exercises.hint_pt: Parágrafo 1: Escolha uma habilidade e explique por quê. Parágrafo 2: Exemplos + conclusão.
exercises.model_answer:
Among all the skills needed for success in the 21st century, I believe adaptability is the most important. The world is changing faster than ever — new technologies emerge constantly, entire industries are being transformed by automation, and the job market demands skills that didn't exist ten years ago. People who can learn quickly, embrace change, and reinvent themselves will thrive, while those who resist change risk being left behind.

History provides many examples of the importance of adaptability. Companies like Nokia and Blockbuster failed because they could not adapt to new technologies, while companies like Netflix and Amazon succeeded by constantly evolving. On a personal level, the most successful professionals I know are those who continuously learn new skills and are willing to step outside their comfort zones. In conclusion, while skills like communication and critical thinking are important, adaptability is the foundation that allows us to develop all other skills as the world around us changes.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Ensaio com tese forte: I believe... + exemplos históricos + conclusão

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva um ensaio curto (2 parágrafos):
exercises.prompt: Should Brazil invest more in public transportation or in roads for cars?
exercises.hint_pt: Parágrafo 1: Argumento principal. Parágrafo 2: Contra-argumento + conclusão.
exercises.model_answer:
Brazil should prioritize investment in public transportation over building more roads for cars. The country's major cities are already suffering from severe traffic congestion, and building more roads has been shown to be a temporary solution at best — new roads quickly fill up with more cars, a phenomenon known as 'induced demand.' Modern public transit systems like subways, light rail, and bus rapid transit can move thousands of people efficiently and with lower environmental impact than individual cars.

Some argue that Brazil's size and geography make cars necessary, especially in regions with poor infrastructure. This is a fair point for rural areas, but it does not apply to large urban centers where most Brazilians live. Cities like Curitiba have demonstrated that well-designed bus systems can be affordable and efficient. Moreover, investing in public transportation benefits low-income citizens the most, as they are less likely to own cars. In conclusion, while some road investment is necessary, Brazil would benefit far more from a comprehensive public transportation strategy.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Ensaio persuasivo: tese → evidência → contra-argumento → refutação → conclusão

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva um ensaio curto (2 parágrafos):
exercises.prompt: Describe the importance of learning English for Brazilian university students.
exercises.hint_pt: Parágrafo 1: Por que inglês é importante. Parágrafo 2: Como isso afeta a carreira + conclusão.
exercises.model_answer:
English has become the global language of science, business, and technology, making it an essential skill for Brazilian university students. The vast majority of academic research is published in English, and students who cannot read these papers are at a significant disadvantage. International conferences, exchange programs, and scholarships almost always require English proficiency. Furthermore, English opens doors to a wealth of online resources — from free courses on platforms like Coursera and MIT OpenCourseWare to the latest professional content on LinkedIn and YouTube.

The career benefits of English proficiency are equally significant. Multinational companies operating in Brazil consistently list English as a requirement for management positions, and bilingual professionals earn an average of 30-50% more than their monolingual peers. For medical students specifically, English is crucial for accessing the latest research, applying for international residencies, and communicating with colleagues at global conferences. In conclusion, learning English is not optional for Brazilian university students who want to compete in an increasingly globalized world — it is a fundamental requirement for academic and professional success.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Ensaio acadêmico: contextualização → argumentos com dados → exemplos específicos → conclusão forte

theme: Escrever parágrafos com causa e efeito

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva um parágrafo (4-5 frases) explicando causa e efeito:
exercises.prompt: What are the causes and effects of air pollution in big cities?
exercises.hint_pt: Estrutura: 1) Introduza o problema 2) Dê 2 causas (because, due to) 3) Dê 2 efeitos (as a result, consequently) 4) Conclusão
exercises.model_answer: Air pollution is a major problem in big cities around the world. The main causes are vehicle emissions and industrial factories, which release harmful gases into the atmosphere. Due to the high concentration of pollutants, many residents suffer from respiratory diseases such as asthma and bronchitis. As a result, hospitals in polluted cities see a significant increase in patients with breathing problems. Consequently, governments are investing in public transportation and renewable energy to reduce air pollution levels.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Causa: because, due to, since. Efeito: as a result, consequently, therefore.

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva um parágrafo de causa e efeito:
exercises.prompt: What happens when students use their phones too much?
exercises.hint_pt: Causas (por que usam tanto) → Efeitos (o que acontece)
exercises.model_answer: Many students spend excessive time on their phones due to addictive social media apps and constant notifications. This excessive screen time has several negative effects. First, students who use their phones late at night often suffer from poor sleep quality, which affects their concentration in class. Second, spending hours on social media can lead to anxiety and low self-esteem, as students constantly compare themselves to others. As a result, many schools have implemented phone-free policies during class hours to help students focus on their studies.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Causa: due to, because of. Efeito: as a result, which affects, can lead to.

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva sobre causa e efeito:
exercises.prompt: Why is obesity increasing in many countries and what are its consequences?
exercises.hint_pt: Causas (dieta, sedentarismo) → Consequências (saúde, custos)
exercises.model_answer: Obesity rates have been increasing worldwide due to a combination of unhealthy diets and sedentary lifestyles. Processed foods high in sugar and fat have become cheaper and more accessible than fresh, nutritious options. Additionally, many people spend long hours sitting at desks or in front of screens, getting little physical exercise. The consequences of this trend are serious: obesity increases the risk of heart disease, type 2 diabetes, and certain cancers. Furthermore, the economic burden on healthcare systems is enormous, with billions of dollars spent annually on obesity-related treatments.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Causa: due to, because. Efeito: the consequences are, increases the risk of, furthermore.

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva sobre causa e efeito:
exercises.prompt: What are the causes and effects of deforestation in the Amazon?
exercises.hint_pt: Causas (pecuária, agricultura, mineração) → Efeitos (biodiversidade, clima)
exercises.model_answer: Deforestation in the Amazon rainforest is primarily caused by cattle ranching, which accounts for approximately 80% of forest clearing. Soybean farming, illegal logging, and mining also contribute significantly to the destruction. The effects of this deforestation are far-reaching. First, thousands of plant and animal species lose their habitats, threatening biodiversity. Second, the Amazon plays a crucial role in absorbing carbon dioxide, so its destruction accelerates climate change. Finally, indigenous communities who depend on the forest for their livelihoods are displaced and their cultures are threatened.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Causa: is caused by, contribute to. Efeito: the effects are, threatens, accelerates.

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva sobre causa e efeito:
exercises.prompt: How does lack of sleep affect university students?
exercises.hint_pt: Por que não dormem (estudos, redes sociais) → Como afeta (notas, saúde, humor)
exercises.model_answer: Many university students do not get enough sleep because of heavy academic workloads, part-time jobs, and excessive use of social media at night. This chronic sleep deprivation has significant consequences for their academic performance and health. Students who sleep less than six hours per night have difficulty concentrating during lectures and retaining information, which leads to lower grades. Moreover, lack of sleep weakens the immune system, making students more susceptible to illnesses. It also affects mood and mental health, increasing the risk of anxiety and depression. Therefore, universities should educate students about the importance of healthy sleep habits.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Causa: because of, due to. Efeito: leads to, making (gerúndio causal), therefore.

theme: Escrever parágrafos de vantagens e desvantagens

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva um parágrafo discutindo vantagens e desvantagens:
exercises.prompt: What are the advantages and disadvantages of studying abroad?
exercises.hint_pt: Estrutura: Intro → 2 vantagens → 2 desvantagens → Conclusão pessoal
exercises.model_answer: Studying abroad offers both significant advantages and challenges. On the positive side, students gain international experience, improve their language skills through daily immersion, and develop independence by living away from their families. These experiences are highly valued by employers and can open doors to global career opportunities. On the other hand, studying abroad can be very expensive, especially in countries like the United States and the United Kingdom. Students may also experience homesickness and culture shock, which can affect their emotional well-being and academic performance. Despite these challenges, I believe the benefits of studying abroad outweigh the drawbacks, as the personal and professional growth it provides is invaluable.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Estrutura: On the positive side... On the other hand... Despite... I believe...

exercises.instruction_pt: Discuta vantagens e desvantagens:
exercises.prompt: What are the pros and cons of working from home?
exercises.hint_pt: Vantagens (flexibilidade, sem trânsito) → Desvantagens (isolamento, distrações) → Opinião
exercises.model_answer: Working from home has become increasingly common and presents both advantages and disadvantages. The main benefit is flexibility — employees can create their own schedules, avoid long commutes, and have a better work-life balance. Companies also save money on office space and overhead costs. However, remote work has significant drawbacks. Many workers report feeling isolated and disconnected from their colleagues, which can affect teamwork and motivation. Additionally, the boundary between work and personal life can become blurred, leading some people to work longer hours than they would in an office. In my view, a hybrid model that combines remote and in-office work offers the best of both worlds.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Vantagens: The main benefit is... Companies also... Desvantagens: However... Additionally...

exercises.instruction_pt: Discuta vantagens e desvantagens:
exercises.prompt: Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of social media for students.
exercises.hint_pt: Vantagens (comunicação, informação) → Desvantagens (distração, saúde mental) → Sua opinião
exercises.model_answer: Social media plays a significant role in the lives of modern students, offering both opportunities and risks. Among the advantages, social media facilitates communication with classmates and professors, provides access to educational content, and allows students to build professional networks through platforms like LinkedIn. Students can also join study groups and share academic resources easily. Nevertheless, social media poses serious risks to students. The constant flow of notifications is a major distraction that reduces study time and concentration. Furthermore, research has linked excessive social media use to increased anxiety, depression, and sleep problems among young people. In conclusion, while social media can be a valuable tool for learning, students need to use it mindfully and set clear boundaries to avoid its negative effects.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Estrutura equilibrada: Among the advantages... Nevertheless... In conclusion...

exercises.instruction_pt: Discuta vantagens e desvantagens:
exercises.prompt: What are the advantages and disadvantages of electric cars?
exercises.hint_pt: Vantagens (meio ambiente, custo de combustível) → Desvantagens (preço, recarga) → Opinião
exercises.model_answer: Electric cars are becoming increasingly popular as the world seeks to reduce carbon emissions. One of the main advantages is that they produce zero direct emissions, making them much better for the environment than gasoline-powered vehicles. Electric cars are also cheaper to operate, as electricity costs less than gasoline and the vehicles require less maintenance. On the downside, electric cars are still significantly more expensive to purchase than conventional cars, putting them out of reach for many consumers. Another disadvantage is the limited charging infrastructure — in many areas, charging stations are scarce, and fully charging a battery can take several hours. Despite these limitations, I believe electric cars represent the future of transportation and that their disadvantages will diminish as technology improves.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Vantagens: One of the main advantages is... On the downside... Despite...

exercises.instruction_pt: Discuta vantagens e desvantagens:
exercises.prompt: What are the pros and cons of gap years before university?
exercises.hint_pt: Prós (experiência, maturidade) → Contras (perder ritmo, custo) → Opinião
exercises.model_answer: Taking a gap year between high school and university has become an increasingly popular choice among young people, and it comes with both benefits and risks. Supporters argue that a gap year provides valuable life experience, helps students discover their interests, and prevents burnout. Many students use this time to travel, volunteer, or gain work experience, which can make them more mature and focused when they eventually enter university. However, critics point out that students who take a gap year may lose their study habits and find it difficult to return to academic life. There is also a financial consideration, as traveling or volunteering for a year can be expensive. On balance, I think a well-planned gap year can be extremely beneficial, but only if the student uses the time productively rather than simply taking a long vacation.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Estrutura: Supporters argue that... However, critics point out... On balance, I think...

theme: Escrever emails formais em inglês

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva um email formal pedindo informações sobre um programa de intercâmbio:
exercises.prompt: Write a formal email to a university's international office asking about their exchange program for medical students.
exercises.hint_pt: Estrutura: Dear Sir/Madam → motivo do email → perguntas específicas → agradecimento → Sincerely
exercises.model_answer:
Dear Sir or Madam,

I am writing to inquire about the exchange program for international medical students at your university. I am currently a third-year medical student at the University of São Paulo in Brazil, and I am interested in spending one semester at your institution.

I would be grateful if you could provide me with information regarding the following: the application deadline for the upcoming academic year, the required English proficiency scores (TOEFL or IELTS), any available scholarships for international students, and the clinical rotations offered to exchange students.

Thank you for your time and attention. I look forward to hearing from you.

Sincerely,
Pedro Silva
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Email formal: Dear Sir/Madam, I am writing to..., I would be grateful if..., Sincerely

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva um email formal para um professor:
exercises.prompt: Write an email to a professor asking for an extension on an assignment deadline.
exercises.hint_pt: Seja educado: explique o motivo + peça a extensão + agradeça
exercises.model_answer:
Dear Professor Williams,

I hope this email finds you well. I am writing to respectfully request an extension for the research paper that is due on Friday, November 15th.

Unfortunately, I have been dealing with a family emergency this week that has significantly limited my study time. I have completed approximately 70% of the paper and believe I can deliver a much better quality submission if given an additional three days.

I understand that deadlines are important and I apologize for any inconvenience. If an extension is not possible, I will submit what I have completed by the original deadline.

Thank you for your understanding.

Best regards,
Ana Santos
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Pedido formal: I am writing to request..., Unfortunately..., I apologize for..., Thank you for...

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva um email candidatando-se a um estágio:
exercises.prompt: Write a formal email applying for a summer internship at a hospital in the United States.
exercises.hint_pt: Apresente-se + por que quer o estágio + suas qualificações + disponibilidade
exercises.model_answer:
Dear Dr. Johnson,

I am writing to express my interest in the summer internship program at Massachusetts General Hospital. I am a fourth-year medical student at the Federal University of Minas Gerais in Brazil, with a strong interest in internal medicine.

During my studies, I have completed clinical rotations in cardiology, neurology, and emergency medicine. I have also participated in a research project on cardiovascular disease in underserved populations, which was presented at a national medical conference. I am fluent in Portuguese and have an advanced level of English (TOEFL iBT score: 98).

I am available from June 1st to August 31st and would be honored to contribute to your team while gaining valuable international clinical experience.

Please find my CV and recommendation letters attached. I look forward to the opportunity to discuss my application further.

Sincerely,
Marcos Oliveira
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Candidatura: I am writing to express my interest..., During my studies..., I am available..., Please find attached...

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva um email de reclamação formal:
exercises.prompt: Write a formal email to a language school complaining about the quality of an online English course you purchased.
exercises.hint_pt: Descreva o problema + o que esperava + o que quer (reembolso/solução)
exercises.model_answer:
Dear Customer Service,

I am writing to express my dissatisfaction with the Advanced English Online Course (Order #45892) that I purchased on October 1st for $199.

The course was advertised as including live video classes with native teachers, personalized feedback on writing assignments, and access to a conversation practice platform. However, I have experienced several issues: the live classes have been canceled three times without notice, I have not received any feedback on the two essays I submitted, and the conversation platform has been offline for the past two weeks.

I would appreciate either a full refund or a transfer to a course that delivers the services as advertised. I have attached screenshots documenting these issues.

I expect a response within five business days. Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Sincerely,
Julia Ferreira
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Reclamação: I am writing to express my dissatisfaction..., However..., I would appreciate..., I expect...

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva um email agradecendo:
exercises.prompt: Write a thank-you email to a professor who wrote you a recommendation letter for a graduate school application.
exercises.hint_pt: Agradeça + mencione o que a carta significa para você + atualize sobre o resultado
exercises.model_answer:
Dear Professor Martinez,

I am writing to sincerely thank you for taking the time to write a recommendation letter for my graduate school application to Columbia University.

Your support throughout my undergraduate studies has been invaluable, and I know that your recommendation carried significant weight in the admissions process. I am delighted to inform you that I have been accepted into the Master's program in Public Health, starting next fall.

I truly believe that your mentorship and guidance during my research project were instrumental in this achievement. I would not have reached this milestone without your support.

Once again, thank you for everything. I hope to make you proud.

With gratitude,
Carla Rodrigues
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Agradecimento: I am writing to sincerely thank you..., I am delighted to inform..., With gratitude

theme: Ensaios acadêmicos — Introdução + Desenvolvimento + Conclusão

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva um ensaio curto (3 parágrafos) com introdução, desenvolvimento e conclusão:
exercises.prompt: Should governments invest more in preventive healthcare or in treating diseases? Discuss.
exercises.hint_pt: Parágrafo 1: Introdução (contextualização + tese). Parágrafo 2: Argumentos + evidências. Parágrafo 3: Conclusão.
exercises.model_answer:
Healthcare systems around the world face a fundamental choice: should limited resources be directed primarily toward preventing diseases or treating them after they occur? While both approaches are essential, I believe that governments should prioritize investment in preventive healthcare, as it is more cost-effective and leads to better long-term outcomes for the population.

Preventive healthcare includes vaccination programs, public health education, regular screening tests, and initiatives to promote healthy lifestyles. Studies consistently show that every dollar invested in prevention saves between two and ten dollars in treatment costs. For example, a comprehensive childhood vaccination program costs a fraction of what it would cost to treat the diseases it prevents. Similarly, public campaigns encouraging exercise and healthy eating can significantly reduce rates of obesity, diabetes, and heart disease — conditions that are extremely expensive to treat. Countries like Finland and Japan, which have invested heavily in preventive care, consistently rank among the healthiest nations in the world.

In conclusion, while treating existing diseases will always be necessary, governments that invest more in prevention can improve population health while reducing overall healthcare spending. A balanced approach that prioritizes prevention without neglecting treatment is the most effective strategy for building a healthier society.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Ensaio acadêmico: Tese na introdução → Evidências no desenvolvimento → Reafirmação na conclusão

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva um ensaio de 3 parágrafos:
exercises.prompt: Is artificial intelligence a threat or an opportunity for the job market?
exercises.hint_pt: Intro com tese → Desenvolvimento com ambos os lados → Conclusão com sua posição
exercises.model_answer:
Artificial intelligence is rapidly transforming industries across the globe, sparking a heated debate about its impact on employment. While some fear that AI will lead to massive job losses, others argue that it will create new opportunities and enhance human productivity. I believe that AI represents both a challenge and an opportunity, and its ultimate impact will depend on how societies prepare for the transition.

On one hand, AI and automation are already displacing workers in manufacturing, customer service, and data entry. A widely cited study by McKinsey estimates that up to 30% of current jobs could be automated by 2030. This is particularly concerning for workers in routine, repetitive positions who may lack the skills to transition to new roles. On the other hand, AI is creating entirely new categories of jobs — data scientists, AI ethicists, machine learning engineers, and prompt designers are roles that barely existed a decade ago. Furthermore, AI can augment human capabilities rather than replace them, allowing professionals to focus on creative, strategic, and interpersonal tasks that machines cannot perform.

In conclusion, while the transition to an AI-driven economy will undoubtedly be disruptive, it does not have to be destructive. Governments and educational institutions must invest in retraining programs, update curricula to include digital literacy and critical thinking, and create safety nets for displaced workers. With the right policies, AI can become a powerful tool for economic growth and human development rather than a source of inequality.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Ensaio balanceado: Both sides presented → Evidence → Personal position with nuance

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva um ensaio de 3 parágrafos:
exercises.prompt: Should English be a mandatory subject from first grade in Brazilian schools?
exercises.hint_pt: Intro + argumentos a favor com evidência + contra-argumento + conclusão
exercises.model_answer:
Brazil ranks among the lowest in English proficiency in Latin America, despite English being the global language of science, business, and technology. This raises an important question: should English be made a mandatory subject starting from the first grade in all Brazilian schools? I strongly believe it should, as early language education is more effective and would give Brazilian students a significant competitive advantage.

Research in linguistics consistently shows that children who begin learning a second language before age seven develop better pronunciation, greater fluency, and stronger cognitive skills compared to those who start later. The critical period hypothesis suggests that the brain's ability to acquire language naturally decreases after puberty. Countries like the Netherlands, Sweden, and Denmark, where English education begins in early childhood, consistently produce citizens with near-native English proficiency. If Brazil adopted a similar approach, the impact on international competitiveness, scientific output, and economic development could be transformative.

Critics may argue that Brazil lacks enough qualified English teachers for universal early instruction, and this is a valid concern. However, technology can help bridge this gap through AI-powered language learning apps, online tutoring platforms, and teacher training programs. The investment required is substantial but justified by the long-term returns. In conclusion, making English mandatory from first grade is not just an educational reform — it is a strategic investment in Brazil's future.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Ensaio persuasivo: Tese forte → Evidência científica → Contra-argumento + refutação → Conclusão forte

exercises.instruction_pt: Reorganize as partes para formar um parágrafo coerente:
words: However, some people prefer online classes.
Education has changed a lot in recent years.
Both methods have advantages and disadvantages.
Traditional classrooms are still popular.
exercises.correct_sentence: Education has changed a lot in recent years. Traditional classrooms are still popular. However, some people prefer online classes. Both methods have advantages and disadvantages.
exercises.explanation_pt: Um parágrafo bem organizado segue: introdução → fato → contraste (However) → conclusão.

exercises.instruction_pt: Complete com o conector adequado:
exercises.sentence: I studied hard for the exam. _____, I didn't pass.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Unfortunately' (infelizmente) indica resultado negativo inesperado. 'Fortunately' = felizmente. 'Moreover' = além disso. 'Therefore' = portanto.

theme: TOEFL Independent Writing — ensaio de opinião (300+ palavras)

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva um ensaio no estilo TOEFL Independent Writing (300+ palavras, 30 minutos). Dê sua opinião e suporte com razões e exemplos.
exercises.prompt: Do you agree or disagree with the following statement? 'It is better to have a broad knowledge of many academic subjects than to specialize in one specific subject.' Use specific reasons and examples to support your answer.
exercises.hint_pt: TOEFL format: 1) Introdução com tese clara 2) Parágrafo corpo 1 (razão + exemplo) 3) Parágrafo corpo 2 (razão + exemplo) 4) Conclusão
exercises.model_answer:
In today's increasingly complex and interconnected world, the question of whether to pursue broad knowledge or deep specialization is more relevant than ever. While both approaches have merit, I believe that specializing in one specific subject is generally more beneficial than having a broad but superficial understanding of many fields.

First, specialization allows individuals to develop genuine expertise, which is essential for making meaningful contributions to their field. A doctor who has spent years studying cardiology will be far more effective at treating heart disease than a general practitioner with surface-level knowledge of many specialties. Similarly, a software engineer who specializes in cybersecurity will be better equipped to protect organizations from increasingly sophisticated cyber threats. In a competitive job market, employers value depth of knowledge and the ability to solve complex, field-specific problems.

Second, the most significant innovations and breakthroughs in human history have come from specialists who devoted their careers to understanding a single domain deeply. Marie Curie's groundbreaking discoveries in radioactivity, for example, were the result of years of focused, specialized research. While a broadly educated person might appreciate the significance of these discoveries, they would lack the depth of understanding necessary to make them.

Admittedly, having some breadth of knowledge is valuable for understanding how different fields connect and for communicating with colleagues from other disciplines. However, this broad understanding can be acquired through general education and personal curiosity, without sacrificing the depth that comes from specialization.

In conclusion, while a broad education provides a useful foundation, true professional success and the ability to advance human knowledge depend on the kind of deep, focused expertise that only specialization can provide.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: TOEFL essay: Tese clara na intro → Body 1 (reason + example) → Body 2 (reason + example) → Counter + refutation → Conclusion restating thesis

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva um email curto pedindo informações sobre um curso de inglês.
exercises.scene_pt: Você quer saber: preço, horários e se tem aulas online.
exercises.template:
Dear Sir/Madam,
I am writing to _____ about _____.
Could you please _____?
I would also like to know _____.
Thank you for your time.
Best regards,
exercises.model_answer:
Dear Sir/Madam,
I am writing to inquire about your English course.
Could you please tell me the price and the schedule?
I would also like to know if you offer online classes.
Thank you for your time.
Best regards,
[Name]

exercises.instruction_pt: Escolha a palavra formal correta para o email:
exercises.sentence: I am writing to _____ about the available positions.
exercises.explanation_pt: Em emails formais, 'inquire' é mais apropriado que 'ask'. 'Inquire' = perguntar formalmente.

exercises.instruction_pt: Organize as frases do email na ordem correta:
words: Best regards, Ana
I look forward to hearing from you.
Dear Mr. Johnson,
I am writing to apply for the internship position.
exercises.correct_sentence: Dear Mr. Johnson, I am writing to apply for the internship position. I look forward to hearing from you. Best regards, Ana
exercises.explanation_pt: Estrutura de email formal: Saudação → Motivo → Encerramento → Despedida.

exercises.instruction_pt: Complete com o conector acadêmico correto:
exercises.sentence: The research on formal email writing presents compelling evidence. _____, further investigation is warranted.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Nevertheless' (no entanto) indica que apesar da evidência, mais pesquisa é necessária.

theme: TOEFL Integrated Writing — Leitura + Áudio + Ensaio

exercises.instruction_pt: Leia o trecho acadêmico. Depois, escreva um ensaio de 150-225 palavras explicando como a lecture se relaciona com o texto (concorda, discorda ou complementa).
exercises.prompt:
Reading: Many educators believe that homework is essential for reinforcing what students learn in class. Studies show that students who regularly complete homework perform better on standardized tests. Homework also teaches students important skills like time management and self-discipline.

Lecture summary: The professor challenges the reading by citing research showing that excessive homework causes stress, reduces sleep, and decreases motivation. She notes that in Finland — which consistently ranks among the top education systems — students receive very little homework. She argues that quality of study time matters more than quantity.
exercises.hint_pt: TOEFL Integrated: Não dê SUA opinião. Apenas explique como a lecture responde ao texto. Use: 'The reading states that... However, the professor argues that...'
exercises.model_answer:
The reading and the lecture present contrasting views on the value of homework. The reading argues that homework is essential for academic success, citing studies that link regular homework completion to higher standardized test scores. It also claims that homework develops important life skills such as time management and self-discipline.

However, the professor challenges these claims with compelling counterarguments. First, she points out that excessive homework can actually harm students by causing stress, reducing sleep quality, and decreasing their motivation to learn. Second, she presents the example of Finland, a country that consistently achieves top rankings in international education assessments despite assigning very little homework to students. This directly contradicts the reading's assumption that more homework leads to better academic outcomes.

The professor's main point is that the quality of study time is more important than the quantity. While the reading focuses on the benefits of homework in general terms, the lecture provides specific evidence suggesting that less homework, when combined with effective classroom instruction, can produce equal or superior results.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: TOEFL Integrated: The reading states... The professor argues/challenges/contradicts... NUNCA dê sua opinião pessoal.

exercises.instruction_pt: Integrated Writing (150-225 palavras):
exercises.prompt:
Reading: Genetically modified organisms (GMOs) have been shown to increase crop yields, reduce pesticide use, and enhance nutritional content. Golden Rice, for example, was engineered to contain vitamin A, potentially preventing blindness in millions of children in developing countries.

Lecture summary: The professor argues that the benefits of GMOs are overstated. He says that increased yields have primarily benefited large agricultural corporations, not small farmers. He also notes that the long-term health effects of consuming GMOs have not been adequately studied, and that GMO crops can contaminate neighboring organic farms through cross-pollination, threatening organic farmers' livelihoods.
exercises.hint_pt: Explique como a lecture responde ao texto. Use transições acadêmicas.
exercises.model_answer:
The reading presents a positive view of genetically modified organisms, highlighting their potential to increase crop yields, reduce pesticide use, and improve nutrition. It cites Golden Rice as an example of how genetic engineering can address vitamin A deficiency in developing countries.

The professor, however, raises several concerns that challenge these claims. First, he argues that the economic benefits of GMOs have been unevenly distributed, primarily enriching large agricultural corporations rather than helping small-scale farmers who need them most. This suggests that the reading's optimistic view of GMO benefits may be oversimplified.

Second, the professor questions the safety of GMOs by pointing out that their long-term health effects on humans have not been adequately researched. While the reading focuses on nutritional benefits, the lecture highlights the potential risks that remain unknown. Finally, the professor introduces the problem of cross-pollination, whereby GMO crops can contaminate nearby organic farms, threatening the livelihoods of organic farmers. This environmental concern is not addressed in the reading at all.

In summary, while the reading emphasizes the potential benefits of GMOs, the lecture presents significant economic, health, and environmental concerns that complicate this narrative.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Integrated essay: Reading says X → Lecture challenges with Y → Specific evidence → Summary

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva sua opinião em 3-4 frases:
exercises.prompt: Do you think students should wear uniforms at school? Why or why not?
exercises.hint_pt: Use: In my opinion... First... Additionally... For example...
exercises.model_answer: In my opinion, students should wear uniforms at school. First, uniforms create equality among students regardless of their economic background. Additionally, they help students focus on learning rather than fashion. For example, in many successful schools around the world, uniforms are mandatory.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Expressando opinião: In my opinion, I believe, I think

exercises.instruction_pt: Complete com a expressão de opinião correta:
exercises.sentence: _____, technology has improved our lives significantly.
exercises.explanation_pt: A expressão correta é 'In my opinion' (na minha opinião). Nunca 'on/by/at my opinion'.

exercises.instruction_pt: Organize o parágrafo de opinião:
words: Therefore, I believe exercise is essential for everyone.
Regular exercise has many health benefits.
For instance, it reduces stress and improves sleep quality.
In my opinion, people should exercise at least three times a week.
exercises.correct_sentence: In my opinion, people should exercise at least three times a week. Regular exercise has many health benefits. For instance, it reduces stress and improves sleep quality. Therefore, I believe exercise is essential for everyone.
exercises.explanation_pt: Parágrafo de opinião: opinião → argumento → exemplo → conclusão.

theme: TOEFL Independent Writing — Agree/Disagree (300+ palavras)

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva um ensaio TOEFL Independent (300+ palavras, 30 min). Concorde ou discorde e sustente com razões e exemplos.
exercises.prompt: Do you agree or disagree with the following statement? 'Young people today are less willing to work hard than previous generations.' Use specific reasons and examples to support your answer.
exercises.hint_pt: 4 parágrafos: Intro (tese) → Body 1 (razão + exemplo) → Body 2 (razão + exemplo) → Conclusão
exercises.model_answer:
The claim that young people today are less willing to work hard than previous generations is a common stereotype that I strongly disagree with. While the nature of work has changed significantly, today's young people face unique challenges that require just as much — if not more — effort and dedication as previous generations.

First, the economic landscape facing young people today is considerably more competitive than in the past. Previous generations could often secure stable employment with only a high school diploma. Today, a bachelor's degree is frequently the minimum requirement for entry-level positions, and many careers demand advanced degrees or specialized certifications. This means young people must invest more years in education and training before they can even begin their careers. A medical student, for example, spends approximately 10-14 years in education and training after high school before practicing independently — a level of dedication that is hard to characterize as laziness.

Second, the perception that young people are lazy often stems from differences in work style rather than work ethic. Previous generations valued long hours at a desk as a sign of commitment. Today's young professionals prioritize efficiency and work-life balance, using technology to accomplish in fewer hours what previously took an entire day. Working smarter should not be confused with working less. Moreover, many young people today manage multiple responsibilities simultaneously — studying, working part-time jobs, maintaining a social media presence for their careers, and often dealing with student debt — a juggling act that previous generations rarely faced.

In conclusion, characterizing today's young people as lazy reflects a misunderstanding of modern challenges rather than an accurate assessment of their work ethic. Each generation faces its own obstacles, and today's youth are no exception — they are simply adapting to a different world.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: TOEFL essay: Tese forte na intro → Evidence with specific examples → Acknowledgment of opposing view → Strong conclusion

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva um parágrafo comparando duas opções:
exercises.prompt: Compare living in a big city versus living in a small town. Which do you prefer?
exercises.hint_pt: Use: On one hand... On the other hand... While... In contrast...
exercises.model_answer: Living in a big city offers more job opportunities and entertainment options. On the other hand, small towns provide a quieter lifestyle and stronger community connections. While cities have better public transportation, small towns have less pollution and lower living costs. In my opinion, I prefer living in a small town because I value peace and nature.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Comparação: On one hand/On the other hand, While, In contrast

exercises.instruction_pt: Complete com o conector de comparação:
exercises.sentence: Cities offer many job opportunities. _____, small towns provide a better quality of life.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'In contrast' (em contraste) conecta ideias opostas. 'In addition' = além disso. 'Similarly' = similarmente. 'As a result' = como resultado.

exercises.instruction_pt: Escolha o conector correto:
exercises.sentence: She studied abroad for two years. _____, her English improved significantly.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'As a result' (como resultado) indica consequência. A melhora do inglês é consequência de estudar fora.

exercises.instruction_pt: Organize o parágrafo de comparação:
words: In contrast, online shopping is more convenient but you cannot try items before buying.
In conclusion, both have their advantages.
Shopping in stores allows you to see and touch products.
However, stores have limited hours while online shops are open 24/7.
exercises.correct_sentence: Shopping in stores allows you to see and touch products. In contrast, online shopping is more convenient but you cannot try items before buying. However, stores have limited hours while online shops are open 24/7. In conclusion, both have their advantages.
exercises.explanation_pt: Parágrafo de comparação: ponto A → contraste com ponto B → detalhe → conclusão.

theme: TOEFL Independent — Preferência (300+ palavras)

exercises.instruction_pt: Ensaio TOEFL (300+ palavras). Escolha e defenda.
exercises.prompt: Some people prefer to take classes with a lot of discussion. Others prefer classes where the professor lectures and students listen. Which do you prefer and why?
exercises.hint_pt: Intro → 2 body paragraphs com razões + exemplos pessoais → Conclusão
exercises.model_answer:
When it comes to classroom format, I strongly prefer classes that incorporate significant discussion over traditional lecture-based courses. While both approaches have their merits, I believe discussion-based classes are more effective for deep learning and professional development.

The primary reason I prefer discussion-based classes is that they promote active engagement with the material. In a traditional lecture, students passively receive information, which research shows leads to lower retention rates. A study by the National Academy of Sciences found that students in active-learning environments performed better on exams and were 1.5 times less likely to fail compared to those in lecture-based courses. In my own experience, I remember far more from classes where I had to articulate my thoughts and defend my positions than from classes where I simply took notes while a professor spoke.

Additionally, discussion-based classes develop critical skills that are essential for professional success. When participating in discussions, students must listen carefully to others, formulate responses quickly, evaluate different perspectives, and communicate their ideas clearly and persuasively. These are exactly the skills that employers value most. During my internship at a hospital, the ability to discuss cases with colleagues, consider alternative diagnoses, and articulate my reasoning was far more important than the ability to memorize textbook information. The collaborative skills I developed in discussion-based classes directly prepared me for this real-world environment.

Admittedly, lectures have certain advantages — they are efficient for covering large amounts of material and can be particularly effective when delivered by an expert in the field. However, I believe these benefits can be achieved through recorded lectures that students watch before class, freeing class time for the discussions and activities that truly deepen understanding.

In conclusion, while both formats have a place in education, discussion-based classes better prepare students for the collaborative, analytical demands of modern professional life.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: TOEFL preference essay: Clear choice → Academic evidence + personal example → Counterargument acknowledged → Restate position

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva um parágrafo argumentativo (4-5 frases):
exercises.prompt: Should social media have age restrictions? Defend your position.
exercises.hint_pt: Use: I strongly believe... The main reason... Furthermore... Research shows...
exercises.model_answer: I strongly believe social media should have stricter age restrictions. The main reason is that young children are vulnerable to cyberbullying and inappropriate content. Furthermore, excessive screen time can negatively affect their mental health and academic performance. Research shows that children under 13 are not mature enough to handle online interactions safely. Therefore, platforms should enforce age verification more rigorously.
exercises.grammar_focus_pt: Argumento forte: I strongly believe, The main reason, Furthermore, Research shows

exercises.instruction_pt: Complete com a expressão argumentativa:
exercises.sentence: _____ that renewable energy is the future of our planet.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'It is widely believed' (é amplamente acreditado) é uma expressão formal para introduzir consenso. Voz passiva: is + believed.

exercises.instruction_pt: Escolha o intensificador correto:
exercises.sentence: The results were _____ significant, changing our understanding completely.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Highly significant' é a combinação acadêmica correta. 'Very much' não combina com adjetivos. 'Highly' = altamente.

exercises.instruction_pt: Organize as frases do argumento:
words: In conclusion, the benefits far outweigh the risks.
Moreover, it creates thousands of jobs in the technology sector.
Critics argue that it is too expensive.
Renewable energy reduces pollution significantly.
However, costs have decreased by 80% in the last decade.
exercises.correct_sentence: Renewable energy reduces pollution significantly. Moreover, it creates thousands of jobs in the technology sector. Critics argue that it is too expensive. However, costs have decreased by 80% in the last decade. In conclusion, the benefits far outweigh the risks.
exercises.explanation_pt: Argumento: tese → suporte → contra-argumento → refutação → conclusão.

theme: Ensaio acadêmico com dados e citações

exercises.instruction_pt: Escreva um ensaio acadêmico (300+ palavras) usando dados e referências para apoiar seus argumentos.
exercises.prompt: To what extent should governments regulate social media platforms? Discuss the balance between free speech and public safety.
exercises.hint_pt: Use dados/estatísticas → Cite estudos → Apresente ambos os lados → Posição fundamentada
exercises.model_answer:
The question of whether and how governments should regulate social media platforms has become one of the most pressing policy debates of our time. As platforms like Facebook, Twitter, and TikTok have grown to billions of users, their impact on public discourse, mental health, and democratic processes has become impossible to ignore. I argue that moderate government regulation is necessary to protect public safety while preserving freedom of expression.

The case for regulation is compelling. A 2021 report by the surgeon general of the United States warned that social media poses a 'profound risk' to the mental health of young people, with adolescents who spend more than three hours daily on social media facing double the risk of depression and anxiety. Furthermore, investigations have revealed that platform algorithms systematically promote extremist content because it generates more engagement. During the 2016 and 2020 U.S. elections, coordinated disinformation campaigns on social media reached hundreds of millions of users, potentially influencing democratic outcomes. These are not theoretical concerns — they represent measurable harm to individuals and societies.

However, regulation must be carefully designed to avoid suppressing legitimate speech. The European Union's Digital Services Act provides a useful model: rather than dictating what content is allowed, it requires platforms to be transparent about their algorithms, provide clear terms of service, and take responsibility for the systemic risks their products create. This approach addresses the structural problems — opaque algorithms, lack of accountability, insufficient content moderation — without empowering governments to censor specific viewpoints.

Critics argue that any regulation threatens free speech and innovation. While this concern is valid, it ignores the fact that unregulated platforms have themselves become threats to free speech by amplifying certain voices and suppressing others through algorithmic bias.
